title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a Go client and broker-policy compatibility record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go client and broker-policy compatibility record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Delayed Retry Policy
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 7883 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry, strict priority, and consumer-timeout policy limits; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Delayed Retry Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use rabbitmq 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Delayed Retry Policy, keep Delayed Retry Policy connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feature Version Gate
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-feature-version-gate-vt04o3" that uses case 6485 to enforce RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry, strict priority, and consumer-timeout policy limits. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.
